key_topics: Multiplication and division within 100: meaning and relationship
Multiplication tables (facts) 0-10: fluency
Properties of multiplication: commutative, associative, distributive
Division as equal groups and as unknown factor problems
Word problems involving multiplication and division
Two-step word problems using four operations
Patterns in arithmetic: identifying arithmetic patterns
Number and Operations in Base Ten: rounding to nearest 10 or 100
Fluently adding and subtracting within 1000
Multiplying one-digit numbers by multiples of 10
Number and Operations — Fractions: unit fractions, fractions on a number line
Comparing fractions with same numerator or denominator
Equivalent fractions (introduction)
Measurement and Data: measuring to the nearest half/quarter inch
Area: understanding area, counting unit squares
Perimeter of polygons
Geometry: categorizing shapes by properties (quadrilaterals)
competences: Solve two-step word problems using all four operations
Understand fractions as numbers, represent on number line
Find area by multiplying side lengths
Understand perimeter as a measurement around a figure
typical_mistakes: Confusing multiplication and addition in word problems
Confusing area and perimeter (adding sides for area instead of multiplying)
Not understanding fractions as parts of a whole (thinking bigger denominator = bigger piece)
Forgetting to carry in multi-digit addition

key_topics: Reading Literature: theme, central message, lessons from stories
Determining point of view: author vs. narrator/character
Comparing and contrasting themes and settings of stories
Reading Informational Text: main idea and supporting details
Using text features (headings, captions, glossary) to locate information
Describing the logical connection between sentences and paragraphs
Phonics: decoding multisyllabic words, common Latin suffixes
Reading with fluency, accuracy, and appropriate rate
Writing: opinion essays with introduction, supporting reasons, conclusion
Informative/explanatory writing with facts and details
Narrative writing: sequenced events, descriptive details, dialogue
Research: gathering information from multiple sources
Language: abstract nouns, regular past tense, simple verb tenses, comparative adjectives
competences: Identify theme and main idea in literature and informational texts
Write multi-paragraph opinion, informative, and narrative texts
Use text evidence to support answers
Read third-grade text accurately and fluently (90+ words per minute)
typical_mistakes: Writing opinions without reasons or evidence
Using vague pronouns (it, they) without clear antecedents
Not using transitional words in writing
Difficulty identifying theme vs. summarizing plot

key_topics: Forces and interactions: balanced/unbalanced forces, magnets
Inheritance and variation of traits
Fossils and what they reveal about ancient environments
Weather and climate patterns: seasonal changes, climate data
Impact of weather on humans
Climate solutions: ways to reduce weather-related hazards
Ecosystems: food chains, food webs, interdependence
competences: Investigate forces and their effects on motion
Explain how fossils provide evidence of past life
Describe food chains and food webs in ecosystems
typical_mistakes: Thinking fossils form quickly (not understanding geological time)
Confusing producers, consumers, decomposers in food webs

key_topics: Local and state government structure
State history and geography
Native American cultures and contributions
Economics: supply and demand, scarcity
Geography: using maps to identify regions of the US
Citizenship: rights and responsibilities
Historical figures: people who fought for equality
competences: Describe levels of government (local, state, national)
Identify geographic regions of the United States
Describe contributions of historical figures to American history
typical_mistakes: Confusing state and national government roles
